questions.context: A regular polygon has n sides. Its interior angle is (2n − 4) × 90° ÷ n degrees.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText:
The interior angle of a regular polygon is 156°.
Work out the number of sides.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText:
Two of these regular 15-sided polygons are placed side by side sharing a common edge.
Work out the angle between the two outer edges at their shared vertex.

markScheme.desc: Angle at the shared vertex involves two interior angles and the gap
Angles around a point sum to 360°

workingSteps: At the shared vertex, there are two interior angles of 156° each from the two polygons

questions.context: ABCDEF is a regular hexagon with centre O. M is the midpoint of AB.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Show that triangle OAB is equilateral.
parts.correctAnswer: OA = OB = AB; angle AOB = 60°, isosceles with 60° apex gives all angles 60°.

markScheme.desc: Isosceles with apex 60°: base angles = 60°; all angles 60°, so equilateral

workingSteps: OA = OB (both radii of the circle circumscribing the regular hexagon)
Triangle OAB is isosceles with apex angle 60°
All angles 60°, so all sides equal: OA = OB = AB — equilateral

parts.label: b
parts.questionText:
Work out the distance OM, where M is the midpoint of AB.
Give your answer in terms of the side length s.

markScheme.desc: OM² = OA² − AM² = s² − (s/2)²  = s² − s²/4 = 3s²/4

workingSteps: Since triangle OAB is equilateral with side s, OA = s and AM = s/2
OM² = OA² − AM² = s² − (s/2)² = s² − s²/4 = 3s²/4

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Work out the area of the regular hexagon in terms of s.

markScheme.desc: Area of equilateral triangle OAB = (1/2) × s × (s√3/2) = s²√3/4

workingSteps: Area of one equilateral triangle = (1/2) × base × height = (1/2) × s × (s√3/2) = s²√3/4
